title: rosa agrestis var. pseudoelliptica
intro: Rosa agrestis var. pseudoelliptica, also known as the wild rose or dog rose, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Rosaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The health benefits of Rosa agrestis var. pseudoelliptica include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote skin health. Rosa agrestis var. pseudoelliptica can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, tinctures, and salves, making it a versatile and accessible remedy for various health concerns.
